yesterdayStoryline.headline: Messi, Haaland and Mbappé define Day 6
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Groups I and J opened with heavyweight forwards shaping margins and early tables.

todayStoryline.headline: Groups K and L move into view
todayStoryline.subtitle: Portugal and England start as favourites, while Uzbekistan’s debut gives the night a quieter edge.

yesterday.scorers: Kylian Mbappé 66', 90+6' (FRA) · Bradley Barcola 82' (FRA) · Ibrahim Mbaye 90+5' (SEN)

lines: France needed patience before Mbappé’s second-half finishing broke Senegal’s resistance.
The win puts France level on points in Group I but behind Norway on goal difference.

yesterday.scorers: Aymen Hussein 39' (IRQ) · Erling Haaland 29', 43' (NOR) · Leo Østigård 76' (NOR) · Aymen Hussein 90+6' OG (NOR)

lines: Norway’s direct service gave Haaland early chances, though Iraq briefly exposed space down the right.
The margin sends Norway top of Group I after the opening round.
Argentina controlled territory and Messi supplied all three finishes, including two from range.
The holders lead Group J on goal difference after a clean opening win.

yesterday.scorers: Romano Schmid 21' (AUT) · Ali Olwan 50' (JOR) · Yazan Al-Arab 76' OG (AUT) · Marko Arnautović 90+12' pen (AUT)

lines: Jordan stayed in the contest after Olwan’s historic equaliser, but set pieces tilted the match.
Austria join Argentina on three points in Group J, behind only on goal difference.

facts.home: Ronaldo’s famous “Siu” celebration started in a 2013 friendly against Chelsea.
facts.away: DR Congo arrived in Houston wearing leopard-print suits and matching travel bags.

facts.home: Bellingham’s “Wolf” celebration came from an England card-game joke with team staff.
facts.away: Modrić was filmed herding goats as a five-year-old in a Croatian wolf documentary.

facts.home: Asamoah Gyan recorded hiplife albums as “Baby Jet” between Black Stars goals.
facts.away: Felipe Baloy scored Panama’s first World Cup goal at 37 against England.

facts.home: Uzbekistan’s “White Wolves” hired 2006 Ballon d’Or winner Fabio Cannavaro as coach.
facts.away: Valderrama once dyed his famous golden perm bright pink for charity.

news.headline: Oil surge clouds global growth outlook
news.body: Reuters reported that rising inflation and a roughly 30% jump in oil prices are weighing on global growth forecasts. Leaders of major economies are expected to discuss the strain, though a unified public blame line remains unlikely.

footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs
